items.title: Empathy framing beats motivational framing in mid-summer
items.whatHappened: p003 ('You're not lazy. You're hot.') outperformed the planned tip carousel by 4x on saves. The hook acknowledged the audience's struggle instead of prescribing behavior.
items.lesson: When the audience is already demoralized, leading with empathy + naming the feeling outperforms leading with the solution.
items.recommendedBehaviorChange: For any seasonal slump moment (winter dark months, post-holiday, summer heat), draft 2 versions: empathy-led and tactic-led. Default to empathy-led if engagement is the goal.

items.title: Coach Spotlight format works on LinkedIn but not as a Reel
items.whatHappened: Lena's burnout-to-barbell story landed on LinkedIn (640 likes, 19 comments from HR titles). The same story re-cut as a 60-second Reel underperformed our IG baseline.
items.lesson: LinkedIn rewards long-form vulnerability. IG audiences want the result, not the journey, unless it's a member (not coach) doing the narrating.
items.recommendedBehaviorChange: Reserve coach origin stories for LinkedIn long-form. Use members (not coaches) as the protagonist in any IG narrative content.

items.title: Hard-sell urgency copy fatigues this audience fast
items.whatHappened: Week-6 'last chance' copy on challenge sign-up posts dropped engagement 38% vs week-5 launch copy. Sign-ups in that week missed target by 15%.
items.lesson: Our audience reads 'last chance' as marketing language and disengages. They respond to limits framed as logistics (cohort size, coach capacity) not as scarcity tactics.
items.recommendedBehaviorChange: Replace 'last chance' / 'don't miss out' with capacity-based framing: 'cohort closes at N members' or 'we coach max 8 per group.' Test in week 11 closing window.
